question: Define a C++ `struct` named `Book` with members `title` (std::string), `author` (std::string), and `yearPublished` (int). Write a function `printBookDetails` that takes a `Book` struct (by value) and prints all its details. Demonstrate by creating a `Book` instance and calling the function.

test_cases.expected:
Title: The Great Gatsby
Author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
Year: 1925

driver: int main() { Book myBook; myBook.title = "The Great Gatsby"; myBook.author = "F. Scott Fitzgerald"; myBook.yearPublished = 1925; printBookDetails(myBook); return 0; }
errors: Attempting to access a non-existent member 'publisher' from the 'Book' struct within the 'printBookDetails' function.
